description:
You are an observability engineer who has just finished tweaking a collection
    of Grafana dashboards stored in
    /home/user/observability-dashboard/.  
    Your next job is to publish a **minor** semantic-version bump and write
    the corresponding CHANGELOG entry before the team’s daily build starts.

    Current situation  
    • A project directory already exists at  
      /home/user/observability-dashboard/  
      and contains (among other things) these two files:
      1) /home/user/observability-dashboard/VERSION  
         – single line text file, currently “1.4.2”
      2) /home/user/observability-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md  
         – a Markdown file whose first (top-most) section is

           ## [1.4.2] - 2023-04-27  
           ### Fixed  
           – Corrected alert rule syntax in latency dashboard

    Goal  
    1. Bump the project version from **1.4.2** to **1.5.0**  
       (minor bump: reset patch to 0 and increment minor).  
    2. Add a new, properly-formatted section to the very top of
       CHANGELOG.md that documents today’s change.
    3. Leave everything else in the file unchanged and in the same order.

    Required output format  
    • VERSION must end up as one and only one line containing  
      1.5.0  
      followed by a single trailing newline (UNIX LF).

    • CHANGELOG.md must begin with the following five lines (exact
      characters and spacing; test harness is strict):

      ## [1.5.0] - 2023-05-10  
      ### Added  
      – Increased CPU usage panel resolution from 1 m to 30 s  

      (That is: heading line, a sub-heading line, the bullet line beginning
      with an en-dash and a single blank line afterwards.)

    How to verify (what the grader will check)  
    1. cat /home/user/observability-dashboard/VERSION  
       must print exactly “1.5.0” plus a newline.  
    2. head -n 5 /home/user/observability-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md  
       must match the five lines shown above, byte-for-byte.  
    3. The previous 1.4.2 section must still be present and unchanged
       directly after the blank line you inserted.

    Notes & constraints  
    • You can accomplish everything with two to three normal shell commands  
      (e.g. echo, sed, or printf).  
    • Do NOT delete or rename existing files.  
    • No need to commit to git; only the file contents matter.  
    • You do not have root privileges; everything required is writable by
      the current user.  
    • Feel free to print the file contents to the console when you are done
      so you can visually confirm they are correct.
truth:
Initial filesystem state created by test harness before agent starts:

    1. File: /home/user/observability-dashboard/VERSION  
       Permissions: 0644  
       Contents (single line, no trailing spaces):  
       1.4.2\n

    2. File: /home/user/observability-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md  
       Permissions: 0644  
       Contents:  
       ## [1.4.2] - 2023-04-27\n
       ### Fixed\n
       – Corrected alert rule syntax in latency dashboard\n
       \n

    Expected final filesystem state after successful task completion:

    1. /home/user/observability-dashboard/VERSION  
       Content:  
       1.5.0\n

    2. /home/user/observability-dashboard/CHANGELOG.md  
       New content (combined; marker ␤ denotes LF newline):

       ## [1.5.0] - 2023-05-10␤
       ### Added␤
       – Increased CPU usage panel resolution from 1 m to 30 s␤
       ␤
       ## [1.4.2] - 2023-04-27␤
       ### Fixed␤
       – Corrected alert rule syntax in latency dashboard␤
       ␤

    The grader will open the first five lines of CHANGELOG.md and compare them
    literally with the block listed in the public task description and will
    also ensure that the old section still follows immediately afterwards.
